races.race_name: Future Ticketing Handicap

runners.name: Inferno
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward with second of 13 in 1m Wetherby handicap a week ago; can race off the same mark here so must enter calculations in his bid to get off the mark at the seventh attempt.
runners.comment: Very good second at Wetherby latest; must enter calculations in his bid to get off mark

runners.name: Only Dream Big
runners.spotlight: Much improved under a more positive ride when easily gaining her first win in 7f Redcar handicap three days ago; should prove just as effective over this longer trip and she's the one to beat under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Much improved when making all at Redcar three days ago; big shout under 6lb penalty

runners.name: Kameko Fever
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when fitted with cheekpieces and only 11th in 7f Redcar handicap last month; headgear is dispensed with here so no surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces on when 11th at Redcar latest; headgear is dispensed with so could bounce back
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Thankfully Simmy
runners.spotlight: In decent nick without winning this season until tried in cheekpieces when a below-par sixth of ten to Mereside Princess in 1m Carlisle handicap last month; more is required.
runners.comment: Tried in cheekpieces when below-par sixth at Carlisle last month; more is required

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough win at Carlisle (1m) in May and comfortably followed up over C&D 17 days ago; hiked up 9lb here but she can go well again.
runners.comment: On hat-trick after wins at Carlisle and over C&D; hiked up 9lb but she can go well again

runners.name: Sanny Doo
runners.spotlight: Has offered little on all his five starts to date, including his handicap debut at Ayr on Saturday.
runners.comment: Has offered little on all his five starts to date, including handicap debut on Saturday

runners.name: Singarda
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden who was fitted with a tongue strap when a below-par ninth of 15 in 7f Doncaster handicap eight days ago; he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Tongue tied when below-par ninth at Doncaster eight days ago; he needs to bounce back
runners.owner: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle

races.race_name: 100% Racing TV Profits Back To Racing Selling Stakes

runners.name: Lady Dublin
runners.spotlight: Ardad filly; improved on her third start with a staying-on sixth of 15 in 6f York seller ten days ago; that form rates the pick here and she merits serious consideration stepped up in trip.
runners.comment: Improved with sixth in York seller ten days ago; merits serious consideration up in trip

runners.name: Bluestone Lady
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward in first-time blinkers when a never-nearer fourth of ten in 6f Carlisle maiden on her debut for Phillip Makin (formerly with Karl Burke) a week ago; not taken lightly with headgear again sported stepped up to 7f.
runners.comment: Blinkered when good fourth in Carlisle maiden on debut for new yard; not taken lightly

runners.name: Liveadream
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward from her debut with fourth of 11 in 7f Chester seller nine days ago; she can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Good fourth of 11 in 7f Chester seller nine days ago; she can make her presence felt
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 13 & P J Makin

runners.name: Amantha
runners.spotlight: Looks poor at this stage and she failed to beat a rival in C&D novice 17 days ago; more is needed despite the drop in grade.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in C&D novice 17 days ago; more is needed despite the drop in grade

runners.name: Northern Viola
runners.spotlight: 28-1, hinted at promise when fifth of seven in maiden at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on her debut a week ago; this daughter of Stradivarius should build on it here.
runners.comment: Debut fifth in maiden at Wetherby; this daughter of Stradivarius can build on it here

runners.name: Turbo Shandy
runners.spotlight: 25-1, always behind when a debut ninth of 11 in seller at Chester (7f, good to soft) nine days ago; lots more is required from this Kodiac filly.
runners.comment: Debut ninth in seller at Chester; lots more is required from this Kodiac filly

races.race_name: Tamper-Proof Handicap

runners.name: Viviana
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when third in 6f Redcar maiden in April but she's twice failed to build on it, last of six in 5.5f Wetherby novice on latter occasion; needs this switch to handicaps to spark progress.
runners.comment: Promising start when third in Redcar maiden in April but she's twice failed to build on it

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but he looked an unlucky second over C&D in April and hasn't enjoyed the rub of the green since, short of room last 1f when fifth at Catterick (7f) last time; weighted to go well off a reduced mark with cheekpieces added (hood also refitted).
runners.comment: Unlucky C&D second in April and not enjoyed rub of the green since; weighted to go well

runners.name: Yaaser
runners.spotlight: Not one to rely on but he hinted at a revival with fifth of nine over C&D 16 days ago; a three-time C&D scorer so can't be discounted off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Hinted at revival with C&D fifth latest; a three-time C&D scorer so can't be discounted

runners.name: Patontheback
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D scorer; made an encouraging return with sixth at Catterick (7f) in May but beat one in 7f Redcar handicap since; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Encouraging return with sixth at Catterick but beat one at Redcar since; more needed

runners.name: Supreme Clarets
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he comes here in decent nick, fourth at Wetherby (7f) before suffering a poor run when ninth at Haydock (7f) last month; one to consider.
runners.comment: Remains winless but he comes here in decent nick; one to consider

runners.name: Due Respect
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at Redcar (7f) last July but hasn't proved the easiest to catch right since, last of 11 back at Redcar (7f) 12 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Gained a first win at Redcar last July but hasn't proved the easiest to catch right since

runners.name: Monhammer
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who got back on track with third of eight in 7f classified event at Ayr two days ago; not discounted back in handicap company.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back on track with third at Ayr two days ago; not discounted

runners.name: Jenni
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways over C&D last May but there's been little to enthuse about since, beating just one at Redcar (6f) last month; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways over C&D last May but there's been little to enthuse about since

runners.name: Concert Boy
runners.spotlight: Kept busy and largely in good form without winning on the AW this season; beat one back on turf in 1m1f Carlisle handicap two weeks ago though so has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Beat one back on turf in Carlisle handicap two weeks ago so has a bit to prove
runners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.name: Carolus Magnus
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who followed a good C&D second on his return in April with a below-par sixth in 7f Carlisle handicap last month; not the easiest to predict but he goes very well here so needs considering.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below-par sixth at Carlisle last month; goes very well here so considered

runners.name: Finn Ironside
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner last summer and back in good form of late, third in a big field at Doncaster (7f) before denied a clear run when eighth there (7f) last time; in the mix.
runners.comment: Back in good form, third at Doncaster before denied clear run when 8th there; in the mix

races.race_name: Haysmith By A Nose 60th Birthday Handicap

runners.name: Sophiesticate
runners.spotlight: A three-time C&D winner for Jim Goldie; much better signs for her new yard with sixth of 11 in 1m4f Hamilton handicap 18 days ago when set a lot to do; well in the mix if building on that here.
runners.comment: Much better signs for new yard with Hamilton sixth latest; C&D winner is well in the mix

runners.name: Gemini Man
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over hurdles in May but right back on song to notch a second success of 2026 in 1m2f Newcastle classified event 20 days ago; this return to handicap company demands more though.
runners.comment: Landed Newcastle classified event 20 days ago; this return to h'cap company demands more

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Gained her sole success at Ayr (1m5f) last July and she's back in very good form, a strong-travelling second of ten in 1m3f Carlisle handicap 13 days ago; a likely player nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Back in very good form, second at Carlisle 13 days ago; a likely player nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but he's made a positive start for Jim Goldie and stayed on late into fifth in 1m Hamilton handicap 12 days ago; one to consider back up in trip here.
runners.comment: Stayed on late into fifth at Hamilton 12 days ago; one to consider back up in trip here

runners.name: Star Of Markinch
runners.spotlight: Is 0-22 and she came in a well-held fourth of five in 2m handicap here 17 days ago; others are much preferred.
runners.comment: Is 0-22 and she came in a well-held fourth of five in 2m handicap here 17 days ago

runners.name: Time Turner
runners.spotlight: Looked to be on the up when gaining a first win in 1m4f Newcastle handicap but he failed to back it up under a penalty when ninth at Southwell (1m3f) later in January; freshened up since so he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle but off since Southwell ninth later in January; freshened up since

runners.name: Rosemary May
runners.spotlight: Recorded a breakthrough win at Ayr (1m) last April; out of sorts subsequently until front-running tactics tried again and third of eight in 1m2f Ripon handicap five days ago; goes up further in trip here and weighted to go close off a 5lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Big step back in right direction with Ripon third five days ago; weighted to go close

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: A'Ali filly; posted a good second in 5f Hamilton novice in May but well below par both subsequent runs, only sixth on her handicap debut at Catterick (5f) 23 days ago; remains with few miles on the clock but she needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Good second at Hamilton but well below par both subsequent runs; needs a big step forward
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways when making all over C&D this month; not disgraced when a strong-travelling sixth in 5f Ayr handicap two days ago and must enter calculations.
runners.comment: C&D win this month; not disgraced when strong-travelling Ayr sixth since; in calculations

runners.name: Sands Of Seve
runners.spotlight: Easily best run when all-the-way winner for Robert Cowell in 5f Wolverhampton handicap in March but well held all three runs since; changed hands for 2,200gns since last time and needs this yard switch to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in March; well held all 3 runs since; revival needed on stable debut

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: Is 0-14 and he beat one in 5f Catterick handicap last time; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Is 0-14 and he beat one in 5f Catterick handicap last time; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Realistic Dream
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who has failed to hit top form for her current yard, fifth of nine in 6f Carlisle handicap two weeks ago; more is required if she's to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form for her current yard; more is required if she's to get off the mark

runners.name: Zuffolo
runners.spotlight: Fair 5f scorer who got back on track with second at Redcar (5f) in April; not best drawn when tenth in 5f Thirsk handicap since and remains one to be interested in off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Good second at Redcar and not best drawn when tenth at Thirsk since; not without interest
runners.owner: R R D Saunders And Partner

runners.name: Hard Nut
runners.spotlight: Arrives out of sorts, last of seven over C&D on his return 16 days ago; it's now 13 runs since his C&D success back in 2024.
runners.comment: Arrives out of sorts, last of seven over C&D on his return 16 days ago

runners.name: Fear And Fast
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and he has offered very little in a trio of runs this season, last two over C&D; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Remains winless and he has offered very little in a trio of runs this season; more needed
runners.owner: Let's Be Lucky Racing 38 & Steven Wilson

runners.name: Supremissy
runners.spotlight: Supremacy filly who took a step forward on her fourth start with a free-going third of four in 5f Hamilton novice last month; she may do better still now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Step forward with third of four at Hamilton; may do better still now going into handicaps

runners.name: Sixcor
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer but this veteran has offered little in a trio of handicaps this term, latest over C&D 16 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: C&D scorer but this veteran has offered little in a trio of handicaps this term

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Scored at Catterick (5f) in April; below form since until hinting at a revival with fifth of nine in 5.5f Wetherby handicap one week ago, despite being very slowly away; this C&D winner needs considering.
runners.comment: Hinted at revival with fifth at Wetherby one week ago; this C&D winner needs considering

races.race_name: Stand In What You Stand For Handicap

runners.name: Highland Olly
runners.spotlight: Landed his fifth win on turf, at Ripon (1m, good; made all) in April; ran okay at Hamilton (1m1f, good) 18 days ago when trying to dominate but was pestered; needs to be able to have his own way up front to be seen to best effect and there are others here who may take him on.
runners.comment: Landed his fifth win on turf at Ripon in April; likes to lead but so do others in this

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but got back on track when scoring at Hamilton (1m1f, good to soft) last Wednesday; unpenalised for that win in an apprentices' race and holds obvious claims.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent but got back on track when scoring at Hamilton; same mark here

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Has won three times on turf this year; ran as though feeling the effects of a busy spell when sixth of eight at Ripon (1m2f, good) last Wednesday and is 6lb higher than her last winning mark.
runners.comment: Ran as though feeling the effects of a busy spell when sixth at Ripon last Wednesday.

runners.name: Welbury
runners.spotlight: Scored on his AW debut at Southwell (1m) in March; has progressed since, finishing runner-up in handicaps at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) and Hamilton (8.5f, good), latterly in first-time cheekpieces (retained); still improving and has good claims.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell AW in March and has finished second twice since; good claims

runners.name: Blakefell
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on return to action in a Musselburgh maiden (7f, good) last month following wind surgery; beaten 3l when sixth of ten on his handicap debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; 18-1) 17 days ago; steps up in trip and still unexposed so there may be more to come.
runners.comment: Beaten 3l when sixth on his handicap debut at Doncaster; may be more to come up in trip
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Lorton Valley
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three races at 1m and 1m1f to date; of more interest now handicapping and is worth a market check.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in three races to date; worth market check on handicap debut

races.race_name: Watch On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Up The Jazz
runners.spotlight: Winless since November 2024 and tried in cheekpieces when a below-par sixth of 12 in 1m Redcar handicap last month; needs to bounce back with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces on when below-par sixth at Redcar last month; needs to bounce back

runners.name: On The Bubble
runners.spotlight: It's now 25 runs since her last success and she beat just two over C&D 16 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: It's now 25 runs since her last success and she beat just two rivals over C&D 16 days ago

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Gained his second win of 2026 at Leicester (1m) in May and posted a good third of 12 in 1m Carlisle handicap a week ago; this course winner rates a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good third at Carlisle a week ago; this course winner rates a player off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: More settled without headgear when gaining a first win, over C&D this month; very slowly away when failing to beat a rival in 1m Ayr handicap three days ago though so has it to prove now.
runners.comment: C&D winner but very slowly away when failing to beat a rival at Ayr three days ago
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Lovette
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m winner in 2025; yet to beat a rival in a pair of 1m/1m1f handicaps at Ayr this season though so has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Yet to beat a rival in a pair of Ayr h'caps this season so has plenty to prove

runners.name: Relevant Range
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over 1m on AW in January and has remained largely in decent form since, though raced freely when sixth of 11 at Wolverhampton (9.5f) last month; well in the mix from an easing mark.
runners.comment: Largely in decent form since two AW wins in January; well in the mix from easing mark

runners.name: Keep Me Stable
runners.spotlight: In top form when last seen on the track last summer, completing a course hat-trick (first over C&D); she needs to hit the ground running after ten months off though.
runners.comment: Completed a course hat-trick last summer; needs to hit ground running after ten months off

races.race_name: British EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Roots In Touche
runners.spotlight: Dandy Man filly; £70,000 yearling who was bought for £165,000 at the Goffs Breeze-up; made a promising debut when second in last month's Hilary Needler at Beverley (5f, good to firm; 28-1, winner 13th in the Queen Mary); sharper track here, but an interesting contender.
runners.comment: £165,000 breeze-up buy; fine second in Hilary Needler at Beverley on debut; interesting

runners.name: Miss Tuite
runners.spotlight: Shaped with plenty of promise when runner-up on her debut at Thirsk (5f, good; 28-1); disappointing next time at Nottingham; a bit to prove after that.
runners.comment: Promising second on Thirsk debut but disappointing at Nottingham next time; bit to prove

runners.name: Sable Noir
runners.spotlight: Filly by Blackbeard; second foal; dam 7f/1m winner; worth a market check on debut; outside draw a negative.
runners.comment: Blackbeard filly whose dam won at 7f/1m; worth a market check on debut; stall 8 a negative

runners.name: Warby
runners.spotlight: Filly by Ardad; 62,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to four winners (all over 5f/6f); dam a 7f 2yo winner; an interesting newcomer with a good draw; no surprise if she were to run well.
runners.comment: By Ardad; half-sister to four winners over 5f/6f; well drawn; should go well on debut

runners.name: Calef
runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut run when fourth of seven in a well-contested fillies' novice at Ripon last time (5f, good; second fifth in the Albany, third won in good style next time); interesting contender.
runners.comment: Improved on debut run when fourth in well-contested Ripon novice last time; interesting

runners.name: Minzelle
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling by smart sprinter Minzaal; sent off the 11-10 favourite for her debut at Haydock last month (6f, good to soft) but could finish only third behind the subsequent Chesham sixth after ducking right at the start and pulling hard; contender down in trip and grade.
runners.comment: 450,000gns yearling; 11-10 shot when third on debut (6f); contender down in grade/trip

runners.name: Ma Fille De Reve
runners.spotlight: Filly by Harry Angel; ran as though needing the run when 8.75l last of six on Wetherby debut (5.5f, good to firm; 33-1, showed up well for a long way); better expected.
runners.comment: Harry Angel filly; showed some promise when 33-1 on Wetherby debut; should improve

runners.name: Angel Steps
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; showed up well until fading in closing stages when nearly 7l eighth of nine in a Wetherby novice on debut (5.5f, good to firm; 50-1); should improve on that.
runners.comment: Eighth of nine when 50-1 shot on Wetherby debut; should improve on that

races.race_name: Jimmy Loxam Is 80 Today "Confined" Handicap (For horses Which Have Not Won In 2026 Flat Turf Season)

runners.name: Wen Moon
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have been on soft ground (the other on good); has been beaten less than 2l when third at Carlisle (5f, good) and Ripon (5f, good) on his last two starts; well drawn; a possible.
runners.comment: Three of his four wins have been on soft; good third on last two runs; a possible
runners.owner: Mps Racing, D Shapiro & D Clifford

runners.name: Woodhay Whisper
runners.spotlight: Ex-Jack Jones (bought for 3,600gns in April); all three wins have been on AW; fair run on stable debut when seventh over 6f at Thirsk (good); well drawn and could go well on a rare run at 5f (races mainly at 6f).
runners.comment: 3,600gns buy from Jack Jones; fair seventh on stable debut; quite interesting back over 5f

runners.name: Storm Call
runners.spotlight: Won at Redcar last October (5f, good) off 2lb lower when blinkered for the first time; not quite so good in two runs on the AW since; may need the run on first outing since March.
runners.comment: Redcar winner with blinkers last October; fair efforts on AW last twice; off since March

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: All his wins have been over 5f on fast ground, two of them last season (one over C&D, the other at Beverley); good third at Ripon two runs back (5f); fair chance on going that suits.
runners.comment: Best on fast ground; two wins last term, first over C&D; good 3rd two runs back
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Our Absent Friends
runners.spotlight: Seven wins, on good to soft or slower turf and AW; stable going well and he wasn't disgraced after stumbling at the start when sixth at Ripon last time (5f, good; just behind Tees Aggregates) after a break; any rain would suit.
runners.comment: All wins have been on AW or good to soft/slower; fair run after a break latest; needs rain

runners.name: Polka
runners.spotlight: All three runs on the AW, getting off the mark in a maiden at Newcastle last time (5f); makes handicap/turf debut having been off since February; likely to be better for the run.
runners.comment: All three runs on AW, winning maiden at Newcastle last time; handicap/turf debut

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Has won twice on good ground but other seven wins have been on good to soft/soft; had his ground when third at Nottingham two runs back, and not beaten far when seventh at Thirsk last week (5f, good); on a good mark but needs rain not sun.
runners.comment: Seven of nine wins have been on good to soft/soft; in fair form but ideally needs rain
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

races.race_name: Catterick Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but she has been most consistent on turf this year; runner-up on her last three starts, the last two  over today's C&D; strong chance of breaking her duck.
runners.comment: 0-9 but she has been most consistent on turf this year, second on her last three starts.

runners.name: Long Shot
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but has made the frame in three of his four starts at Redcar this term; knocking on the door and cheekpieces return replacing blinkers; should not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-8 but has made the frame in three of his four starts at Redcar this term.

runners.name: Thehunnebelllegacy
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile maiden; proved more settled and not disgraced after six months off when fifth of eight at this track (7f, good) last month; drops back in trip and one to consider with that run under her belt.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after six months off when fifth at this track last month.
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Elashgar
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in February and runner-up there (5f, AW) in March; not at the same level on turf debut at Nottingham (6f, good) in April, having been gelded during a 50-day break; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut at Wolverhampton in February; not so good on turf debut last time.

runners.name: Our Hero Matty
runners.spotlight: Maiden who regressed during a busy first season; well beaten in his two runs this year and has left Brian Ellison since; his mark continues to fall but best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Maiden who regressed during a busy first season; best watched on stable debut.

runners.name: Saxon Grace
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but has shown she can win a race, doing best when headed late and a close fourth at Carlisle (6f, good) last month; outpaced in first 5f run, on this track 11 days ago, but returns to 6f in first-time cheekpieces; needs to be considered if the headgear has a positive effect.
runners.comment: 0-11 but best race when only headed close home when fourth at Carlisle (6f) last month.

runners.name: Rivera Queen
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but showed a bit more with first-time tongue-tie and blinkers fitted when fourth of seven at Brighton (6f, good) on handicap debut 13 days ago; more required for the win but holds place claims.
runners.comment: 0-4 but showed a bit more when fourth at Brighton on handicap debut 13 days ago.
runners.owner: D Macauliffe, A Don & J Joyce

races.race_name: Congratulations To The Happy Couple EBF Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: In A Hurry
runners.spotlight: Won a Thirsk maiden last June (7f, good); good third over this C&D next time (good to firm) but has been very disappointing on her last four starts (two of them this season); customary hood is absent and first-time tongue-tie goes on; has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Has been very disappointing on her last four starts (two of them this season).

runners.name: Velvet Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row in the spring, over C&D (good) off 3lb lower in the latest; did far too much in front when eighth of ten at Carlisle (7f, good) last Monday but holds claims if her natural enthusiasm can be harnessed.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins in the spring, latest over C&D; just 3lb higher now and holds claims
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who has returned to form with cheekpieces fitted, finishing runner-up over C&D (good) last month and at Musselburgh (7f, good) 17 days ago; her mark is creeping up but she is getting close to another win and needs to be considered.
runners.comment: Finished runner-up over C&D in May and at Musselburgh 17 days ago; has to be considered

runners.name: Yorkshire Queen
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but ran up to her best when third of ten at Musselburgh (7f, good) 17 days ago; needs to show a tad more to get off the mark but has place claims.
runners.comment: 0-8 but ran up to her best when third at Musselburgh 17 days ago; tad more needed

runners.name: Ziggy's Queen
runners.spotlight: 1-21 on turf and showed only modest form on her last two starts at Thirsk and Carlisle last month; all headgear removed and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 1-21 on turf and showed only modest form on her last two starts at Thirsk and Carlisle.

runners.name: Diamont Katie
runners.spotlight: Back on track when getting off the mark on turf at Pontefract (6f, good) in April following wind surgery; placed twice since and she looks sure to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Back on track when getting off the mark on turf at Pontefract in April.
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin

runners.name: Alessia Fernanda
runners.spotlight: Turf wins at 1m and 1m1f; scored on AW at Southwell (1m) in April following six-month break and wind surgery but below that form at Newcastle since; returns to turf but may be best suited by further than 7f.
runners.comment: Won on AW at Southwell in April but below that form at Newcastle since; trip query at 7f

races.race_name: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap

runners.name: Valley Of Flowers
runners.spotlight: No win since completing a three-timer at 1m4f-2m in the autumn of 2023; however, she ran her best race of the year when going close at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last month; has a good run in her back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has won at up to 2m; back to form at Thirsk in May (1m4f); respected back up in trip

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when stepping up in trip at Ripon (2m, good; made all) in April; placed twice since, the latest when third of 13 in a Class 3 handicap at Thirsk (2m, good) last month; back in calmer waters here and has plenty to recommend him.
runners.comment: Made all when stepping up in trip at Ripon in April; placed twice since; calmer waters now

runners.name: Twilight Safari
runners.spotlight: Yet to win under rules; showed some ability at 1m2f on the Flat for previous trainer but nothing worthwhile over hurdles for this yard and was beaten 60l on his return to the Flat at Ripon last month; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Yet to win under rules; beaten 60l on his return to the Flat at Ripon last month.

runners.name: Arrange
runners.spotlight: Latest of four wins came in August 2024; hasn't figured in two runs this year, at York and Thirsk, and although she takes a drop in grade others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Last of four wins came in August 2024; hasn't figured in two runs back this year
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

races.race_name: Racing Again 8th July Handicap

runners.name: War Memorial
runners.spotlight: Winner over 7f at Musselburgh in April (good) and sound run back up to 1m there last time (good); below par tried in cheekpieces in between; can be involved.
runners.comment: Musselburgh winner in April (7f) and good third there last time (1m); can be involved

runners.name: Blue Jay Way
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here last autumn (soft) and also shaped well when fourth over C&D in May (good); not at his best over 5f on AW last time; could go well back up in trip.
runners.comment: 6f winner here last October; good C&D fourth last month; one with a chance

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Ex-Declan Carroll; C&D winner last July (soft); below par at Beverley last week but comes into the reckoning on his two seconds over C&D in May (good).
runners.comment: Ex-Declan Carroll; eighth at Beverley latest but two good C&D runs before that; a possible

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f on Tapeta and 5f (good) at Hamilton this season; fair efforts over 5f/6f on last two starts; pulled hard on last try at 7f, although still a creditable third at Musselburgh in April; not out of it.
runners.comment: Has won over 5f and 6f this season; could go well back up in trip but needs to settle

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D last September, but poor form on other three outings on turf; most of his racing has been on AW and comes into this having won at Wolverhampton three weeks ago.
runners.comment: C&D 2nd last year, but best form, including both wins, has been on AW; still considered

runners.name: Misemerald
runners.spotlight: Not a frequent winner and hasn't been at her best in recent runs so although well handicapped at present, others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: 2-25; not at her best in recent runs; well treated but hard to fancy

runners.name: Rain Cap
runners.spotlight: Four of his seven wins have been here, three over 6f and the other at this trip; should come on for his run at Carlisle a week ago (6f, good) when returning from a 70-day break; can go well.
runners.comment: Four of his 7 wins have been here; should be better for last week's run; should go well

runners.name: Captain Cess
runners.spotlight: Ex-Adrian Wintle; ten-race maiden; sound efforts on last two starts, notably on stable debut when third at Wetherby last week (5.5f, good to firm; 11-8 favourite, not clearest of runs); seems to stay 7f; a possible.
runners.comment: Ex-Adrian Wintle; 0-10; met trouble when third at Wetherby last week (5.5f) for new yard

runners.name: Martin's Brig
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 2025, the second over 7f on good to firm at Wetherby; hasn't done as well this year, although fair efforts the last twice, most recently over 7f at Redcar (good to firm) last month.
runners.comment: Two wins last year, including over 7f on good to firm; not been in such good form in 2026

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: 32-race maiden and hard to fancy on recent form.
runners.comment: 32-race maiden and hard to fancy on recent form

races.race_name: Ronan & Tom Gibbons Memorial Apprentice Claiming Race

runners.name: Delusional
runners.spotlight: 0-7; best run came when a 0.75l third behind Femme Beauty at Killarney on penultimate; could turn that form around on these terms but needs to rebound from poor run at Limerick 12 days ago; new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Not far behind Femme Beauty at Limerick, could turn that around, new headgear on

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: First sign of ability when keeping on for a 3.5l third of 12 on handicap debut at Gowran latest (9.5f, yielding); big chance with a repeat of that; cheekpieces discarded.
runners.comment: Much better run on h'cap debut latest, big chance with repeat of that, cheekpieces off

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m3f handicap at Killarney (good) early last month off 57; beaten off revised mark twice since; ran well for this rider when a 1l third in a 1m Clonmel claimer in April; more appealing than most.
runners.comment: Won h'cap at Killarney last month, went well in claimer for this rider at Clonmel in April

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and yet to really show winning potential; held behind Femme Beauty at Killarney on penultimate; never involved in 1m Cork handicap latest; drops into claiming company now but needs more with blinkers reapplied.
runners.comment: Held by Femme Beauty on Killarney running, drops in grade now but needs more; blinkers

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in 11 starts for Mick Mulvany; not a bad run off 47 at Gowran on latest but poorly treated by these race conditions and probably best watched on debut for new trainer; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: 0-11, not a bad run off 47 latest, not well treated by race conditions, new yard, watch

runners.name: Kart Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and heavy defeats in first two handicaps; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens, heavy defeats first two handicaps, hard to fancy

runners.name: Music And Song
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in five starts so far; beaten 11l and 38l in first two handicaps; rated 40 and impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-5, beaten 11l and 38l in first two handicaps, rated 40 and impossible to fancy

runners.name: Rose Sienna
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly tailed off at 40-1 on debut at Leopardstown last October; off since and has left Johnny Murtagh; can only be watched in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at 40-1 on debut, new yard now, cheekpieces on, can only watch

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden (Median Auction Race)

runners.name: Wonderfulwonderful
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; has won over hurdles and in the first three in five of six runs on turf on the Flat; not beaten far in Listed hurdle at Musselburgh when last seen in January; rated 72 and worth a market check on return in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Consistent Flat form, off since decent hurdles run in Jan', cheekpieces on, check market

runners.name: Nermal
runners.spotlight: Rated 72; runner up second start on AW (1m) in January and filled the same position on return from 119 days off on turf debut at Down Royal (10.5f, good); slight drop in trip shouldn't inconvenience him and likely to be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Runner up last twice, rated 72, solid run on turf debut latest, thereabouts again

runners.name: Sron Na Caise
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts in first two turf maidens at Gowran (9.5f, soft) and Limerick (1m, yielding); disappointing AW run on latest; rated 73 but has to give weight away here and could be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Two okay turf maiden runs, poor on AW latest, has to give weight away here, vulnerable

runners.name: Swing Street
runners.spotlight: Golden Horn gelding; 10,000gns foal, E30,000 yearling; dam four-time winner at 6f-8.5f (best RPR 89); half-sister to 1m4f AW winner Miss Giler (RPR 93) & 2m Flat/2m6f hurdle winner Tomorrow's Angel out of 1m2f/1m4f winner (90); market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Golden Horn gelding, dam a four-time winner, market best guide on debut

runners.name: The Monday Club
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army gelding; unraced dam closely related to four-time 7f-1m winner Morning Approach (best RPR 77); likely best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Invincible Army gelding, dam unraced, likely best watched on debut unless backed

runners.name: Camino Lad
runners.spotlight: Offered something to build on when a keeping on 8.5l third at Limerick on debut 12 days ago (1m, gd-yld); that run, and his pedigree, suggests that this longer trip will suit and Keane takes over now; should go well.
runners.comment: Shaped like further would suit on debut at Limerick, Keane takes over, should go well

runners.name: Time Whisperer
runners.spotlight: Late gains into seventh of 15 when 66-1 on debut at Gowran (1m, gd-yld), catching the attention of the stewards (rider reported he ran green before staying on); this longer trip should suit on that evidence and he's an interesting runner.
runners.comment: Caught attention of stewards when midfield on debut, longer trip a plus, interesting

races.race_name: Lodge At Ashford Castle Maiden

runners.name: Mano Chicago
runners.spotlight: Twice placed at two; missed all of last year; second in two turf maidens this year, and also a fair fifth of 16 in a competitive handicap at Cork; a bit too keen when not quite failing to match his best form on AW debut at Dundalk last month; good overall credentials for this.
runners.comment: A bit too keen at Dundalk last time, has a good chance here on official and RPR figures
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Harana
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly; promising debut second in 1m Gowran maiden last year, absent for 13 months until third in 1m4f Roscommon maiden, weakened late; prominent for a long way before fading into fifth on handicap debut over 1m1f at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Promising debut second in 1m maiden last year, two fair runs this term, could go close
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Coul Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-10 on the Flat, placed in a Clonmel maiden last season; best of three runs this term when a staying-on third over 1m in a Killarney maiden last month; down the field in a Leopardstown handicap last time.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, well exposed on the Flat, place chance on his best form
runners.owner: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings

runners.name: Take Me
runners.spotlight: Showed ability last year, though failed to improve after a strong-finishing fifth of 18 at Navan (1m2f, good) on the second of four starts; reported coughing after a below-par run on seasonal debut at Gowran; showed a tendency to hang right when unplaced at Dundalk last time.
runners.comment: Reported coughing on seasonal debut, looked ill at ease on AW last time, fair overall form

runners.name: Western Bandit
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame and possibly flattered by an official rating of 73; finished 2 3/4l behind Take Me at Dundalk last time.
runners.comment: Possibly flattered by an official rating of 73, finished 2 3/4l behind Take Me at Dundalk

runners.name: Drowsy Maggie
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to two winners; dam was of no account but a half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Aspen Darlin.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners; dam was of no account but a half-sister to 6f 2yo Group

runners.name: Mischievous Fun
runners.spotlight: Has a useful Flat pedigree but no sign of ability; tailed off in an academy hurdle last December; dismal run in a Killarney bumper last month.
runners.comment: Has a useful Flat pedigree, no sign of ability, tailed off in a hurdle race and a bumper

runners.name: Believe Again
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a Listowel bumper last September, in rear on Flat debut at Roscommon; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a bumper, in rear on Flat debut at Roscommon, can be ruled out.

runners.name: Cuckaloo Hill
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in low-grade handicaps last season and fourth in a C&D handicap three starts back; no apparent chance in a maiden.
runners.comment: Capable of useful handicap form in a modest grade, no apparent chance in a maiden.

runners.name: If Not For Grace
runners.spotlight: 100-1, never in the hunt when last of 15 on debut at Gowran three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 100-1, never in the hunt when last of 15 on debut at Gowran three weeks ago.

runners.name: Big Dispute
runners.spotlight: Struggled after winning a bumper on debut in 2023; staged a revival last summer when placed in three maiden hurdles; has made the frame twice over hurdles since finishing around 10l sixth of 16 behind subsequent Royal Ascot handicap winner Enceladus at Cork on his first run on the Flat
runners.comment: Bumper winner, fair hurdles form last year, may step up from a first Flat run at Cork

races.race_name: Michael Joyce Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Marmeladova
runners.spotlight: Went close in a 1m Navan maiden on the third of five starts last season; a revelation on reappearance over C&D, winning a maiden very easily from an 85-rated gelding; has since faced a stiff task when the outsider of four in a conditions race won by the subsequent Group 3 winner Sparan Nua; could be a handicap blot here.
runners.comment: Ran in good company last time, strong chance based on her wide-margin C&D maiden win

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed soft ground last year, runner-up over 1m at Galway, went one better on heavy at Listowel; below best this season; first-time blinkers failed to produce sufficient improvement for him to feature at Gowran last time, though not beaten far.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner early last season, also won a Listowel handicap, below best this term

runners.name: Grey Leader
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy; tailed off on debut for this stable in the Irish Lincoln; again struggled when dropped in class at Limerick; trainer/rider combined for a win at Limerick last Friday.
runners.comment: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy, has struggled on his first two runs for this stable

runners.name: Merlin The Wizard
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain in 2023 at up to 1m; regressive in 2024 and 2025 seasons; produced best run for some time at Dundalk in March when good second over 1m; finished third to Lough Leane over C&D three starts ago; took advantage of a drop in grade when winning a claimer at Limerick 12 days ago; mark left unchanged.
runners.comment: Mark remains unchanged after a claiming race win, good chance with confidence boosted

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: All six wins in Britain came on the AW; first success on turf came last month over C&D, quickening off a slow pace, despite racing keenly; put a blot on his record when running no sort of race in a Leopardstown event in which Daler finished fifth.
runners.comment: Ran no sort of race at Leopardstown, previously gained a first turf win over C&D

runners.name: Justenzia
runners.spotlight: Now 13lb higher than winning at Clonmel last September; runner-up in her first two starts this term; tailed off at Gowran; no reassurance from her more recent Leopardstown run.
runners.comment: Started the season with two second placings, doubts raised by her most recent outings

runners.name: Dark Viper
runners.spotlight: Has recorded his only two wins over 1m at Leopardstown; his record of finishing second ten times strikes a note of caution; below best in four 2026 appearances.
runners.comment: Both wins gained at Leopardstown, unplaced in four 2026 outings, hard to fancy

runners.name: Storm Averted
runners.spotlight: Has shown her best form at Galway; lacks the benefit of a recent run and may need this on seasonal/stable debut; might be one for the Galway festival in due course.
runners.comment: Has shown her best form at Galway, lacks the benefit of a recent run and may need this

runners.name: Daler
runners.spotlight: Got first career win with a comfortable 1m Dundalk handicap success last October off 72; mixed form on AW during the winter;  unplaced at Naas last month before winning at Leopardstown, his first turf win at the ninth attempt; 7lb rise may anchor him.
runners.comment: AW scorer, opened his turf account at the ninth attempt on latest, 7lb rise may anchor him

runners.name: Mocking
runners.spotlight: Fair placed form early last season and won at Roscommon in the autumn; two 2026 runs can be upgraded a little, has met trouble in running both times.
runners.comment: Maiden winner last autumn, two 2026 runs can be upgraded a little, has met trouble

runners.name: Dark Summit
runners.spotlight: Winner in France; fair effort when set a stiff task on stable debut in the Irish Lincoln; weak form since then, including twice in races of a similar standard to this.
runners.comment: Winner in France, fair stable debut in the Irish Lincoln, weak form since then

races.race_name: J.J. Burke Peugeot Handicap

runners.name: Onyeisi
runners.spotlight: AW winner for Charlie Johnston and generally reliable; signs that he is returning to form gradually; took fourth of 14 at Fairyhouse last time; not sure to reverse previous form with Connova.
runners.comment: Fairly consistent when with Charlie Johnston, signs that he is returning to form gradually

runners.name: Tavarua
runners.spotlight: First of her three runs was her best; that race has been well-advertised by the winner Bobby McGee, who has since completed a hat-trick; visored for the first time on handicap debut.
runners.comment: First of her three runs was her best, that race well advertised by the winner Bobby McGee

runners.name: Sands Castle
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 10l in fifth at Sligo on sole handicap attempt; has plenty of weight here; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Beaten around 10l in fifth on sole handicap attempt, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Wedding Year
runners.spotlight: Weak turf form last season; showed improved form in three outings at Dundalk between November last and April; failed to maintain that progress back on turf at Gowran three weeks ago, leaving him with plenty to find with Rodeo Blues and A Pretty Penny.
runners.comment: Showed improved form at Dundalk, failed to maintain that progress back on turf last time

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: Low-rated filly from a top stable in flying form; two handicap runs have been satisfactory; fourth of 12 at Gowran with A Pretty Penny in fifth; not beaten far in sixth in a 17-runner contest at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Low-rated filly from a top stable in flying form, two handicap runs have been satisfactory
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Brave Approach
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three maidens before making a winning handicap debut at Dundalk in March (1m); that seems to have been a flash in the pan; 33-1 when in rear over 1m4f at Fairyhouse on latest.
runners.comment: Dundalk win in March seems to have been a flash in the pan, in rear on latest

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish in the first three, but fourth on three occasions and does not need to improve much to win a race in this grade;  creditable display when beaten only 2 1/2l in fourth at Leopardstown last Thursday; reserve.
runners.comment: Does not need to improve much to win a race, creditable display last Thursday, reserve

runners.name: A Pretty Penny
runners.spotlight: Hints of ability at Dundalk during the winter; showed improved form to gain a front-running C&D success last month; went up 6lb and finished just out of the money last time, 1 1/4l behind fourth-placed Rodeo Blues.
runners.comment: Opened her account with a front-running C&D success last month, unplaced off revised mark

runners.name: Sand Art
runners.spotlight: Took a small step in the right direction when seventh of 14 at Down Royal last time; not far behind Honey Dale then but both need to improve.
runners.comment: Took a small step in the right direction at Down Royal last time, more needed

runners.name: Canon Law
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Gowran on his only nursery start; reappearance at Gowran looked too bad to be true, finished a remote last of 12 and renews rivalry with Rodeo Blues (fourth) and A Pretty Penny (fifth).
runners.comment: Ran well on his only nursery start, reappearance at Gowran looked too bad to be true

runners.name: Honey Dale
runners.spotlight: Fair fifth over 7f at Down Royal last time; not much between her and Sand Art on that running; both need to find a bit extra.
runners.comment: Fair fifth at Down Royal last time, not much between her and Sand Art on that running

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Never better than fourth in 11 outings; needs to find extra on first run beyond 1m; stable's cuurent form is reasonably encouraging.
runners.comment: Never better than fourth in 11 outings, needs to find extra on first run beyond 1m

runners.name: Connova
runners.spotlight: Could make her presence felt with improvement from handicap/seasonal debut at Gowran; met some trouble in running before staying on into fourth; has an edge over Onyeisi (seventh).
runners.comment: Could make her presence felt with improvement from handicap/seasonal debut at Gowran

runners.name: Flagstone Lady
runners.spotlight: Mild encouragement from her most recent maiden attempt; the bar is set low for her handicap debut, and Colin Keane takes the ride again after partnering at Gowran last time.
runners.comment: Mild encouragement from her most recent maiden attempt, bar is set low for handicap debut

runners.name: Perggosa
runners.spotlight: Maiden form over shorter distances is not good enough to inspire any confidence for her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Maiden form over shorter distances is not good enough to inspire any confidence

races.race_name: GAIN The Advantage Series Handicap

runners.name: Lagoon Nebula
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; managed a couple of good runs in staying handicaps on the Flat last season; not far behind Sonic Invader when fourth at Listowel last time.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, stays well on the Flat, not far behind Sonic Invader at Listowel

runners.name: Eternal Echo
runners.spotlight: Bumper/maiden hurdle winner for Gordon Elliott; ran over shorter trips in maidens; slipped up at Gowran on handicap debut; premature to write off.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdle winner, slipped up at Gowran on handicap debut, premature to write off

runners.name: Miss Australie
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt in 1m4f Roscommon handicap last month (yielding-to-soft), coming from off the pace; never threatened at Limerick off her revised mark.
runners.comment: Opened her account at Roscommon on her 15th start, well beaten off her revised mark

runners.name: Miss Gitana
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins, twice in Britain and over 2m at Tramore last July for Gordon Elliott; yet to hit form for this yard, held by Sonic Invader and Leguna Nebula on Fairyhouse running.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, yet to hit form for this yard, held by Sonic Invader and Leguna Nebula

runners.name: Coulstys Way
runners.spotlight: In good form last summer, winning off 12lb lower at Roscommon (1m4f, good) and placed on four occasions; should be better for her recent return at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Consistent filly last season, should be better for her recent run at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Makaiah
runners.spotlight: Started last season brightly with three placed efforts before a 1m5f Navan win; has also begun this season on a positive note when runner-up at Roscommon; only one win from 22 starts (along with six seconds); worth considering if getting a run; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, one win/six seconds from 22 starts, bright start to the season last month

runners.name: Sonic Invader
runners.spotlight: Showed little in four maiden runs at up to 1m4f; appreciated the step up to 1m6f at Gowran (good to yielding) for his second handicap start when keeping on for third; 2m may have stretched his stamina when third again at Listowel; that was still a good run and gives him a slight edge over Lagoon Nebula.
runners.comment: Third placings at Gowran and Listowel imply a fair chance of picking up a staying handicap

runners.name: Miss Crinshawn
runners.spotlight: Maiden has displayed winning potential on all three starts this year; long-priced third on AW in January; not beaten far at Limerick in April, and went close at Cork last month; has changed stable; one for the short list if getting in; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, maiden has displayed winning potential on all three starts this year

runners.name: Daboya
runners.spotlight: Won a Curragh handicap last season (1m4f, good) and later won a maiden hurdle; needs to raise her game following fifth placings at Roscommon and Gowran.
runners.comment: Versatile sort, needs to raise her game following fifth placings at Roscommon and Gowran

runners.name: Guy's Girl
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in maidens last year at 1m/1m1f; however, never closer than fifth in seven outings and needs to find extra to feature here.
runners.comment: Never closer than fifth in seven outings, needs to find extra to feature here

runners.name: Barrogstown Girl
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2024, winning three handicaps, showing a liking for good ground; failed to score last year and now back on last winning mark; hard to be confident after a poor run over hurdles last month; should be sharper now.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a fine 2024 campaign, failed to score last year, now back on last winning mark

runners.name: Master Sydney
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' bumper at Roscommon last July (good); fair effort on Flat handicap debut at Down Royal (1m5f, yielding); 1m2f too sharp on seasonal return; Roscommon sixth was satisfactory; had a valid excuse (almost slipped up) when fancied at Gowran on latest.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, had a valid excuse (almost slipped up) when fancied at Gowran on latest

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Won twice in France early in his career; Irish Flat form provides little encouragement; however, he showed he was in good shape when winning a handicap hurdle at Downpatrick last month, adding to a novice win at the same venue last summer.
runners.comment: Handicap hurdle winner last month, Irish Flat form not at the level he showed in France

runners.name: Covert Legend
runners.spotlight: 1-21 record in Britain is a negative; limited turf experience, fair AW form, including when second of six over 1m3f at Southwell last October on his final British start; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, 1-21 record in Britain is a negative, limited turf experience, fair AW form

runners.name: Wolf Prince
runners.spotlight: Useful 120-rated hurdler with five wins to his name, the last two gained back-to-back for Fergal O'Brien in April/March 2024; after a long absence, was entitled to need the run at Navan in April on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Useful 120-rated hurdler, was entitled to need the run on his stable debut in April

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 2023; regressive form this year; mid-field last time at Fairyhouse when ridden by Colin Keane who deserts him to ride stablemate Sonic Invader; blinkered first time.
runners.comment: Two wins in 2023, regressive form this year, Colin Keane deserts him to ride a stablemate
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Metamorphic
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly put in her best effort when third in a heavy ground Gowran fillies' maiden last October over an extended 1m1f; reportedly got upset in the stalls on both handicap starts at Dundalk later in the year; risky in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Got upset in the stalls in two handicap starts at Dundalk late last year, seems risky

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Stellar Quality
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden winner (good) at just short of this trip last year; left modest seasonal return behind with promising 4.5l third of 16 on handicap debut at Gowran (1m4f, yielding); frame claims again off the same mark here.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, promising run on h'cap debut latest, same mark now, frame claims

runners.name: Thatwilldoso
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps last season at 9.5f-1m4f and went up a cumulative 18lb in the weights; yet to hit form in two runs this term but has been eased 3lb but would have liked to see a bit more at Leopardstown last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Three handicap wins last season, below best so far this term, others preferred

runners.name: Deressa
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dermot Weld filly made an underwhelming start for this yard in a Curragh handicap (1m2f, good) last month; should be sharper now but has to prove she stays this far; tongue tie and visor go on.
runners.comment: Low key start for this yard latest, up in trip, tongue tie and visor go on now

runners.name: La Dame Blanche
runners.spotlight: 3-27 on turf on the Flat; last win came over C&D (soft) in July 2024 off 11lb higher; hasn't really shaped like a winner in waiting in two runs this season; her last run at Roscommon wasn't too bad but more needed to get her head back in front and she's 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Last won over C&D in July 2024 off 11lb higher, not a bad run latest but more needed

runners.name: Earthsong
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent filly got off the mark at the eighth attempt in a 1m6f (gd-yld) handicap at Gowran last month; raised 7lb for that win and moves up in class; will need to improve again to follow up but that isn't impossible.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Gowran latest, up 7lb and this is harder, needs to improve again

runners.name: Denim And Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden winner (on good ground) has failed to fire in two runs at Gowran this season, though this is more realistic than the Listed race she contested there last time; a drying track would aid her cause but needs to revive.
runners.comment: Good ground maiden winner, this more suitable than latest, drying track a plus

runners.name: Royal Hollow
runners.spotlight: Talented dual-purpose performer is 1-7 on the Flat; solid effort over timber at Listowel last month and ran well off this mark when last seen on the level at Clonmel last September; easy ground fine and should go well.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, fit from hurdling, ran well off this mark on last Flat start, go well

runners.name: Switch From Diesel
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper and hurdles performer won her sole start on the Flat in a 1m4f (heavy) Curragh maiden last November; easy maiden and novice wins over timber at Punchestown on her last two starts and could be on a lenient mark for Flat handicap debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, won last two starts over hurdles, could be on nice mark for h'cap debut

races.race_name: Royal Windsor Supports Racing Staff Week Apprentice Handicap (Apprentice Training Race Series)

runners.name: Alashos
runners.spotlight: Sole turf win came in a Lingfield maiden but he won his first AW race in April (6f) and has since run well in a slightly stronger race than this over C&D (good to firm); has other quite solid handicap form  at this trip on grass and he should go well under up-and-coming Harry Vigors.
runners.comment: In good form, first AW win in April before close 4th in a slightly stronger C&D race

runners.name: Just King High
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner as 2yo; went handicapping off a high mark and his 3yo season was a write off; on a lowly mark now but although he's been thereabouts in AW races at 7f this year he needs more for the return to 6f on turf; has been wearing blinkers and cheekpieces, now goes in a visor.
runners.comment: High in weights for most of 2025; thereabouts over 7f on AW this year; needs more

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on Newcastle AW in February (5f); ran well back on turf when runner-up at Nottingham (6f, good) in April but not so good in final two starts for Brian Ellison; sold for £1,000 since; regular cheekpieces left off on yard debut.
runners.comment: Won on AW in February; good turf run (6f) in April; lesser form after; sold £1,000 since

runners.name: Blindfold Games
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; on the premises in AW sprint handicaps in midwinter; has had excuses in two turf runs since, getting loose before the start on the first occasion and then stretched by 7f on soft at Yarmouth recently; still time to for her to do better back at 6f; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 0-9; on the premises in AW sprints; had excuses on turf since; still time to do better

runners.name: Giles Glory
runners.spotlight: Gained first win over 7f on Lingfield AW in January but he hasn't been finding much over that trip on Tapeta and turf for new trainer; however, the return to 6f looks worth a go.
runners.comment: Won at 7f (AW) in January; weak finisher for new yard; worth another go at 6f

runners.name: Beelzebub
runners.spotlight: All three wins at 6f and 7f on artificial surfaces; on a losing run of over two years but he acts on turf and is on a useful mark for his latest new yard on the pick of his 2025 form; usually wears aids but none today.
runners.comment: All 3 wins on AW; on a losing run but acts on turf; on a useful mark for latest new yard

runners.name: Lahina Bay
runners.spotlight: Three wins at both sprint trips on this course, on good to firm and good; one to keep an eye on as she's been a regular winner down the years but she came home in her own time on her recent return from a break so another run or two might be in order.
runners.comment: Three course wins; one to keep in mind but she finished remote back from layoff last time

runners.name: Gal
runners.spotlight: Modest qualifying form in Ireland; 12-1 for recent handicap and yard debut at Bath (extended 5f, good) following a layoff but there was some promise in her third of 11; should improve, perhaps past a few of these.
runners.comment: Modest 2yo form; creditable show on handicap/yard debut at Bath after layoff; can improve

runners.name: Giant
runners.spotlight: Two handicap wins at 7f and 1m on AW and he ran to a useful level in a better race than this at Southwell in April; hasn't come near breaking his duck on turf on last two starts but he did run well in a better 6f race at Chester last summer so can't be totally dismissed.
runners.comment: Both handicap wins on AW (7f and 1m); has 6f form on turf but poor on last 2 starts

runners.name: Nordic Glory
runners.spotlight: Better known on AW; can lead but he came from off the pace when winning at Bath (5f, firm) last month under James Glennon; nothing went right from a wide draw at Beverley since but he does act well on fast ground and has a generally good record over C&D (course winner at 5f); thereabouts.
runners.comment: Likes quick ground; got up late to win at Bath under J Glennon; badly hampered since

runners.name: Cape Toronada
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her first two handicap starts when stretching out nicely to win at Brighton (6f, good) two weeks ago; raised 6lb but the return to this more conventional track, where she hinted at ability in a very strong C&D novice last summer, might also help; solid claims.
runners.comment: Improving and stretched out nicely when off the mark at Brighton; up 6lb; can do better

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Newcomers' "Confined" Maiden Stakes (Unraced) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Or Ever
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Mehmas half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Kindergarten (RPR 90); dam unraced sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner/Melbourne Cup runner-up Tiger Moth, half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Coach House, out of 6f Group 3 winner; not discounted.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Kindergarten (RPR 90); dam from good family; not discounted

runners.name: Stoney Wood
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros foal, 35,000euros yearling; sixth foal; Cotai Glory half-brother to four winners including Azure Blue (5f/6f including 2yo/Group 2) and Dapper Guest (7f; RPR 96); unraced dam closely related to 1m Group 3 winner Pincheck; Oisin Murphy has good 21% strike-rate for yard; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Half-brother to four winners; Oisin Murphy has good 21% strike-rate for the yard

runners.name: Isca Bay
runners.spotlight: 54,000euros yearling, £40,000 2yo breeze-ups; by Havana Grey; first foal; dam 5f winner (including 2yo; RPR 98), half-sister to two winners notably Meu Amor (7f Listed), out of 7f 2yo winner (85); this North Yorkshire-trained filly may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: £40,000 2yo breeze-ups; dam 5f winner; may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Perfect Nation
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling, 100,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; by Sioux Nation; second foal; dam Group-placed 5.7f-1m1f maiden (RPR 91), half-sister to 5f winner Gwendolyn, out of dual German 7f Listed winner; trainer has healthy 24% strike-rate with 2yos here; interesting, all the more so if well backed.
runners.comment: 100,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; yard has healthy 24% strike-rate with 2yos here; interesting

runners.name: Bollengo Boy
runners.spotlight: 58,000gns yearling; 16th foal; Bayside Boy half-brother to five winners including Heeraat (5f/6f including 2yo/Group 3) and Ambiance (4.5f-6f including 2yo and Swedish Listed); already gelded; trainer has unearthed some useful 2yos this season and he could give a good account.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners; trainer has unearthed some useful 2yos this season

runners.name: Horndean
runners.spotlight: 120,000euros yearling; second foal; dam French Listed-placed 1m3f winner (RPR 96), half-sister to Spanish 1m4f Listed winner Again Charlie; the betting could be informative regarding this Hello Youmzain newcomer.
runners.comment: 120,000euros yearling; dam useful on the continent; the betting could be informative

runners.name: Minzaal Time
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros foal, £135,000 yearling; eighth foal; Minzaal half-brother to five winners including Ancient Times (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 97) and Imperial Trooper (7.3f 2yo; 81); likely to improve for the run but market support might indicate a big performance is coming.
runners.comment: £135,000 yearling; may need this run but market support might indicate a big performance

runners.name: Never Enough
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 5f/5.7f winner (including 2yo debut/Listed) from talented family; it's likely this No Nay Never newcomer will benefit from the run but he's not ruled out in view of his pedigree; check the betting.
runners.comment: Likely he'll benefit from the run but he's not ruled out in view of his pedigree

runners.name: Sultan Darius
runners.spotlight: 230,000gns foal, 120,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; fifth foal; Starspangledbanner brother to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Cheerupsleepyjean (RPR 95); dam unraced half-sister to 5f/6f winner Jack Flash (87), out of 5f 2yo winner (66); trainer won this in 2023; wears tongue-tie; the betting may guide.
runners.comment: 120,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; yard won this in 2023; wears tongue-tie; the betting may guide

runners.name: Lilywhite Hart
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Too Darn Hot filly; dam 5f-7f winner for connections (including 2yo Listed; RPR 103), half-sister to Listed-placed 10.6f-2m winner Tyson Fury, out of useful 6f 2yo winner; connections are also responsible for 260,000gns breeze-up purchase Ahoy; one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: Dam 5f-7f winner for connections (including 2yo Listed); one to watch in the betting

runners.name: Yankeedoodledandy
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Land Force half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Hot To Dot (RPR 77); dam 6f winner (70), half-sister to five winners; others preferred.
runners.comment: Third foal; Land Force half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Hot To Dot (RPR 77); others preferred

runners.name: Flann Sunna
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling; by Blue Point; second foal; dam 5f/6f 2yo winner (including Listed), half-sister to three winners, out of 7f 2yo/1m winner (RPR 87); stable is 3-12 with 2yos here in the last five years; chance.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling; stable is 3-12 with 2yos here in the last five years; chance

runners.name: Public Affection
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros yearling; seventh foal; Showcasing brother to 6f 2yo winner Love Affairs (RPR 85), half-brother to three winners notably Brussels (Group-placed 6f 2yo; 111); dam French 6.5f (Listed) and 1m AW winner; trainer has had winning 2yo newcomers recently at 14-1 and 11-1; could go well.
runners.comment: 110,000euros yearling; trainer has had winning 2yo newcomers recently at 14-1 and 11-1

runners.name: Ahoy
runners.spotlight: 105,000euros yearling, 260,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; ninth foal; Naval Crown half-brother to three winners notably Cosmic Vega (7f/1m including 2yo/Listed) and The X O (Group-placed 6f AW; 106); trainer 0-25 with 2yos on turf in the last five years but he's worth a second look give his purchase price.
runners.comment: Stable 0-25 with 2yos on turf in last 5 years but worth a second look given purchase price

runners.name: Cutlass
runners.spotlight: 72,000euros yearling; second foal; Blackbeard half-brother to French 6f winner Tanques; dam once-raced (RPR 69) half-sister to three Group 3 winners, out of 1m4f Listed winner; likely to improve for this debut outing.
runners.comment: 72,000euros yearling; half-brother to French 6f winner; likely to improve for the run

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & DAB Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: House Of Medici
runners.spotlight: Made favourite for Kempton AW debut (7f) in May where a promising-looking challenge failed to gain momentum; he still kept on quite well though and looks sure to improve for the experience and extra furlong.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on 7f AW debut but still showed promise; can do better over 1m

runners.name: Fiefdom
runners.spotlight: Never seen with a chance on Lingfield debut but showed more when upped to about 1m on Wolverhampton AW (100-1) recently; looks open to more improvement, perhaps when handicapping after today.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability when upped to about 1m at Wolverhampton on second start; handicaps soon

runners.name: Brocklesby Bill
runners.spotlight: Hooded both starts; down the field on 1m2f debut at Newbury (200-1) but his next run at Chepstow (7f, good) was in a different league as he was ridden more prominently and kept on well to get a narrow verdict; may well improve further despite the penalty.
runners.comment: Down the field on Newbury debut; ridden more prominently when narrow winner latest (7f)

runners.name: Star Banner
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of inexperience but also plenty of promise when third of five on Musselburgh debut and perhaps found his next race coming too soon 11 days later when beaten 2f out at Lingfield (also 7f) this month; no surprise to see him repeat former promise now up in trip.
runners.comment: Ran green on 7f debut but showed promise in 3rd; not so good since; may fare better here

runners.name: Electric Gambler
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; second foal; by Galiway; dam runner-up 1m1f in France, half-sister to winners Hertford Dancer (11.6f Listed; RPR 104), Sunpass (1m2f) and Anglo Irish (1m2f-1m4f, AW); already gelded and tongue tied for debut; market can guide for jumps yard.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to Listed winner; gelded and tongue tied on debut; market can guide

runners.name: Roccapina
runners.spotlight: Second foal; by Advertise; dam placed 9.5f/1m3f in France (RPR 73), half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Liquid Amber and to dam of Group 2/3 winner Passenger; market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to Group 3 winner at 1m as 2yo; market can guide on debut

runners.name: Kaiko
runners.spotlight: Always remote after a slow start on Bath debut (1m; 100-1) last month; early days yet.
runners.comment: Always remote after a slow start on Bath debut (1m; 100-1) last month; early days

runners.name: I'mnotinlove
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for Newbury debut over 6f last August where she took the field along but soon cried enough and dropped out; bred to be suited by the step up in trip but unknown quantity after layoff.
runners.comment: Made running but soon faded on 6f Newbury debut; bred to stay this trip; might need this

runners.name: Speckled Brown
runners.spotlight: 28-1, dropped right out on AW debut at Kempton (1m) last October; has left Michael Bell since; lots to prove for new yard.
runners.comment: 28-1, dropped right out on AW debut last October for Michael Bell; can only watch

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Almaty Star
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was found an ordinary Yarmouth handicap when winning easily in the spring; handicapper had his say for that and he finished well beaten behind Dream Composer upped in class at Epsom last time; yard also runs Rhythm N Hooves.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind Dream Composer upped in class at Epsom last time; outside claims

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of his racing over 6f and found his stamina coming into play late when winning a handicap run to suit over C&D three weeks ago; no complaints about a 2lb rise for that but this looks a bit stronger.
runners.comment: Found a handicap run to suit over C&D three weeks ago; no complaints about a 2lb rise

runners.name: Rhythm N Hooves
runners.spotlight: Poor effort at Epsom, behind two of these, last time will need forgiving but he boasts a fair record for Billy Loughnane (won for him last summer off 6lb higher); handicapper given him a chance and it wouldn't be a shock were he to pop up.
runners.comment: Boasts a fair record for Billy Loughnane; handicapper given him a chance; should do better

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Won a less-competitive renewal of this last year under Oisin Murphy (currently 2-2 on him), to further enhance a good course record; arrives in good form, remains on a workable mark and holds decent claims once more.
runners.comment: Won a less-competitive renewal of this last year; in good form and on a workable mark

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner who's tried in a third form of headgear after a few recent runs over 6f (two here); handicapper has given him a chance, dropping him another 3lb, but the cheekpieces will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner; handicapper given him chance but cheekpieces need to make a difference

runners.name: Bassenthwaite
runners.spotlight: Didn't see out 6f when last seen five months ago (since taken out here on account of unsuitable good to soft ground); needs to prove this mark (11lb higher than when making all in an uncompetitive AW nursery last autumn) is within range.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 6f when last seen five months ago; needs to prove this mark is within range

runners.name: Rosario
runners.spotlight: Tendency to blow the start has often cost him; disappointing effort back in the cheekpieces (off again tonight) in the Goodwood handicap he'd won last season early last month, despite a better break; now 3lb lower but will need to better that somewhat.
runners.comment: Tendency to blow the start has often cost him; 3lb lower but needs to bounce back

runners.name: Glamorous Breeze
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, mainly in the summer, who ran as well as she ever has off tonight's career-high mark on her comeback a month ago; entitled to have come on for that at her age and boasts a solid record over C&D (won this in 2023), so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Boasts a solid record over C&D (won this in 2023); enters calculations after solid return

runners.name: Dream Composer
runners.spotlight: In good form for his new yard, faring best of those drawn low in the Epsom Dash last time back in the trademark cheekpieces; bit unlucky at Goodwood on his previous start too and remains on a fair mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: In good form for his new yard and had valid excuses on last two starts; one to consider

races.race_name: Carrington Wealth Management Handicap

runners.name: Edwin Hubble
runners.spotlight: Acquitted himself well in smart company on final 2yo starts at 1m and 1m1f; easy to back for handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) ten days ago where he was checked in his run but nevertheless made some progress from off the pace; can go well with the extra distance likely to suit.
runners.comment: Creditable efforts as 2yo; never involved on recent handicap debut; can do better

runners.name: Astrological
runners.spotlight: Looked a staying type when forging clear at Wolverhampton (extended 1m1f, AW) in April but 1m4f proved a shade too far on Lingfield AW three weeks ago on handicap debut; has improvement in him on turf debut.
runners.comment: Forged clear over 1m1f on AW in April; 1m4f stretched him since; can improve on turf debut

runners.name: Rumbustious
