runners.spotlight: Stayed on well in two of his three 1m2f runs and was well suited by the step up to 1m4f at Thirsk (good) 17 days ago, when he was the only one to press an unexposed winner; solid to go well off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good 2nd to unexposed rival when upped to 1m4f latest; solid chance off the same mark

runners.name: Impossible Mission
runners.spotlight: Successful handicap debut at Wolverhampton (about 1m1f, AW) last November but has had a very mixed record at this level since, albeit this lesser test than at Newbury recently should be in his favour as he is down in grade and back on his winning mark.
runners.comment: Won handicap debut on AW as 2yo; very mixed since; back on winning mark and down in grade

runners.name: Tabasko
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form in four 2yo starts, finding no extra for the switch to a handicap on final start (about 1m1f, AW); reappeared in an ordinary Salisbury handicap (1m2f, good) in May when shaping as though a slightly stiffer test would suit; not far away.
runners.comment: Some 2yo promise; respectable return at Salisbury (1m2f); extra furlong can suit

runners.name: Guitar Solo
runners.spotlight: Unraced as 2yo; best effort on second 7f start, when not far away in a Newbury novice; not a surefire stayer on balance of pedigree now taking a big step up in trip (had been due to make handicap debut over 1m) but at least options improve at this level; market helpful.
runners.comment: Some 7f promise; trip query right up in distance but options improve now handicapping
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Fitzdares Taking Bets Since 1882 Handicap

runners.name: Dreambird Dolly
runners.spotlight: Went very close in first-time tongue-tie/blinkers in Brighton handicap (5.2f, good to firm) last month then made all in a classified at Leicester (5f, good); it took her a long time to post that first win but she came clear with a subsequent winner at Leicester and is unexposed in this headgear combination; can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: It took a long time to post a first win but she's unexposed in this headgear combination
runners.owner: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher

runners.name: Honour Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: Had to challenge wide when fourth of 11 over C&D (good to firm) in May and he went close to breaking his AW duck at Lingfield (5f) last time; has won over C&D and every chance he'll be bang there.
runners.comment: C&D winner who nearly broke his AW duck last time and every chance he'll be bang there

runners.name: Chasing Gold
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt in first-time blinkers at Yarmouth (6f, soft) 11 days ago; he faces different conditions this evening but showed promise on fast ground over C&D last June; could have more to offer in the retained headgear and he's respected up 4lb.
runners.comment: Off mark at 15th attempt at Yarmouth; different conditions here but he may be able to cope

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Two wins off higher marks last season, including C&D, and promising third at Bath in April on her stable/seasonal debut; however, her form has gone the wrong since and she has to raise her game.
runners.comment: Promising third at Bath on stable/seasonal debut but form has gone the wrong way since

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: His losing run goes back to October 2024 and he was only fifth at Lingfield (5f, good) last month, but prior to that he was second over this C&D (good to firm) and the return to Windsor could be a plus for this two-time C&D winner; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: The return to Windsor could be a plus for this two-time C&D winner; he's one to consider

runners.name: Wedgewood
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won at Lingfield (5f, AW) in March and was runner-up on her next two starts (5f, AW/firm); below par in a classified at Bath (5.7f, good) last time but could bounce back with a bold show back down in trip.
runners.comment: In good form over 5f prior to lesser run at 5.7f; could bounce back with a bold show

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Close third off this reduced mark at Brighton (6f, good to firm) last month and he can't be discounted; however, this 8yo has been inconsistent this season and was soundly beaten back at Brighton most recently.
runners.comment: Close third at Brighton last month but inconsistent and soundly beaten back there since

runners.name: Chiedozie
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 25 starts and he's unlikely to be making the breakthrough today judged on this year's evidence.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after 25 starts and hard to fancy on this year's evidence

runners.name: Faustus
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when third off 5lb higher at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) in May; this 8yo has finished soundly beaten on both starts since but has been without cheekpieces, which return today and could be key.
runners.comment: Has gone backwards since good 3rd at Lingfield in May; return of cheekpieces could be key

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark on form as recent as February but he got loose and was withdrawn before the start here early last month, and refused to race here subsequently; comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark but refused to race here last time and comes with risks attached

runners.name: Addictive
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo whose mark continues to fall but he's struggled to get competitive this year, following a very long absence.
runners.comment: He's struggled to get competitive this year, following a very long absence

runners.name: Be An Angel
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 17 starts and no return to form at Chepstow on her recent reappearance, while she might be best at 6f; however, she's 7lb lower than when going close here (6f) last August and is not written off.
runners.comment: No return to form on recent reappearance but on a dangerous mark and not written off

runners.name: Rajeteriat
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but he was in pretty good heart on AW last winter when last seen and he's also effective on turf; each-way shout if he's at the top of his game following a break.
runners.comment: 0-15 but in pretty good heart on AW when last seen and he might have an each-way shout

races.race_name: Vnetrix Cyber Security Solutions Handicap

runners.name: Takeitorleaveit
runners.spotlight: Won on nursery debut at Bath (1m, soft) last September and on reappearance there (1m, firm) in April; merely mid-division over this C&D (good to firm) last month but back on song when runner-up at Lingfield (1m1f, soft) last time; in the mix if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Returned to form when second at Lingfield and he can be in the mix if backing up that run

runners.name: Mertoun
runners.spotlight: Well backed when beaten just a short head at 3-1 on handicap debut over C&D (good to firm) four weeks ago; strong claims up 2lb.
runners.comment: Well backed when going close over C&D on handicap debut and has strong claims up 2lb

runners.name: Grey Sands
runners.spotlight: Easy win for Joseph O'Brien at Southwell (1m, AW) in January on second handicap start; another good run when third at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April on stable debut and he was tackling 11.4f when well beaten here last time (turf debut); not written off back down in trip.
runners.comment: Good third on stable debut (1m2f, AW); tackling 11.4f when well beaten here last time

runners.name: Roserunner
runners.spotlight: Won at Lingfield (1m, AW) last December on second start and went close in handicap at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) next time; on a handy mark on that evidence but three lesser runs have followed (including on sole turf outing) and first-time blinkers (replacing cheekpieces) need to prompt a revival.
runners.comment: AW novice winner in December but hasn't shone since going close on h'cap debut in January

runners.name: Palace Artois
runners.spotlight: No impact on first three starts and soundly beaten when seventh of 14 at 15-2 on last month's handicap debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft); improvement needed but she's in top hands and her dam was a Group 1 winner, so things could click at some point; first-time cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Only seventh on handicap debut but in top hands and things could click at some point

runners.name: Lordsbridge Bay
runners.spotlight: He's one to watch in the betting in first-time blinkers (replacing the hood that was adopted last time) but he's struggled since moving into handicaps and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: He's struggled in handicaps and a major turnaround is necessary in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in a row on AW this spring (7f/1m); of interest on that form but not quite at the same level when back on grass at Nottingham (1m, good) last time, and it's possible the AW is her preferred surface.
runners.comment: Of interest on this spring's AW form but not quite at same level back on grass last time

runners.name: Amazing Anita
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but didn't run badly when fourth to an easy winner at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on her stable debut three weeks ago, and the way she kept on suggests the return to 1m could be a positive; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-9 but kept on for 4th over 7f on stable debut and the return to 1m could be a positive

runners.name: Laurasia
runners.spotlight: She took a step in the right direction when fifth of ten at 100-1 on recent handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW); however, she was beaten just over 4l and further improvement is necessary this evening.
runners.comment: Step in right direction when fifth on handicap debut but further improvement is necessary

runners.name: Inclusive
runners.spotlight: Kept on for promising third at Southwell (6f, AW) in January for James Fanshawe but down the field on final outing for that yard and it's been the same story on her three starts for this one; now 0-9.
runners.comment: Down the field on her last four outings and she's now 0-9

runners.name: Feisty Minx
runners.spotlight: Weakened when upped to 1m at Kempton (AW) last month but she kept on well over 7f either side of that, finishing third at Goodwood (good to soft; turf debut) most recently; this trip is worth another go; a possible.
runners.comment: Weakened over 1m last month but kept on well over 7f either side; 1m is worth another go
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Silky Robin
runners.spotlight: Mark eased after a fruitless spell on the AW and made it four wins on the spin on turf when going in off 5lb lower five weeks ago; currently 2-2 round here and it's hard to find any negatives.
runners.comment: Made it four turf wins on the spin latest; 2-2 round here; hard to find any negatives

runners.name: Recon Mission
runners.spotlight: Useful in his younger days but, while he remains consistent, he's been on the downgrade for a while and has only won one of his last 43 starts; better effort on his second run back over C&D last time and this is a bit easier off a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Better on his second run back over C&D last time; this a bit easier off a career-low mark

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: String of costly defeats on the AW early in the year and remains a maiden; has the ability but hung left back on turf last month before not seeing it out on soft ground last time; risks involved running over 5f for just the second time.
runners.comment: Costly to follow; has the ability but risks involved running over 5f for the second time

runners.name: Desdemona
runners.spotlight: Returns to turf for the first time in a year on a more sensible rating; issues at the start have been costing her of late and much will depend on how she gets away from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Issues at the start have been costing her of late; much will depend on how she gets away

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Useful 3yo who's come back to form since dropped to 5f on turf, minus the blinkers, following up his Lingfield win off 3lb lower with a solid effort at Newbury; needs considering but wants a proper test at this trip and a bigger field would have helped.
runners.comment: Useful 3yo who's come back to form since dropped to 5f on turf, minus the blinkers; player
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Havana Jag
runners.spotlight: Picked up for 4,200gns in the spring from Dylan Cunha, for whom she proved consistent on the AW; has gone both ways under pressure before and rarely seen out her races, so has questions to answer on just her second turf start.
runners.comment: Gone both ways under pressure before and rarely seen out her races; debuts for a new yard

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Roxa Love
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to a couple of decent sprinters in Natural Force and Mademoiselle, plus three lesser lights; easy to back but shaped with promise behind a useful winner on her Lingfield (turf) debut; sets the standard on that.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise behind useful winner on her Lingfield (turf) debut; sets the standard

runners.name: Ava Louise
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns foal, 4,000euros yearling who's a half-sister to six winners, notably the yard's Spiritual Lady (Listed; successful on 2yo debut); has been found a suitable race if she's anywhere near as good as that one.
runners.comment: Half-sister to six winners notably the yard's Spiritual Lady; market will guide

runners.name: Targa
runners.spotlight: £105,000 breeze-up 2yo who completely blew the start on her debut before finishing well behind Roxa Love at a big price last time; it may be in nurseries that she makes her mark in due course.
runners.comment: Well behind Roxa Love last time; it may be in nurseries that she makes her mark

runners.name: Clueisinthetitle
runners.spotlight: Started a big price for her soft-ground debut early in the month and finished beaten a long way having raced with the pace early against the favoured rail; will need to improve a lot on that.
runners.comment: Started a big price for her soft-ground debut early in the month, when beaten a long way

runners.name: Henley On Thames
runners.spotlight: £125,000 breeze-up 2yo whose trainer's strike-rate with juveniles is up this year; among the favourites for his Sandown debut ten days ago, when showing up well for a long way, and is entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Among the favourites on debut, when showing up well for a long way; entitled to improve

races.race_name: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes

runners.name: Foinix
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who ran his best race in a while here a fortnight ago (1m2f, hit traffic late on); one of the likelier winners back to the usual blinkers from a visor and back up in trip.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who ran his best race in a while here a fortnight ago; leading claims
runners.owner: M Robinson, Jack Pook & Jeanette May

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: Solid effort back on turf early last month but below that level on both starts since, most recently when well beaten over C&D yesterday; nearly three years since her last win.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D yesterday and it's been nearly three years since her last win

runners.name: Fighting Poet
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, all in late summer; pleasing comeback early last month but below par here a fortnight ago, when well behind Foinix, and needs to bounce back (has never been the most consistent).
runners.comment: Three-time course winner, all in late summer; needs to bounce back (never been consistent)

runners.name: Dream Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's still to get involved in a finish after seven runs (7f-1m3f); inclined to pull hard a couple of times and is still to conclusively prove her stamina.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard a couple of times and is still to conclusively prove her stamina

runners.name: Hello Garda
runners.spotlight: Irish trainer doesn't bring many across to run on the Flat (0-20 down the years); this gelding has shown very little at huge prices in three runs over shorter and would want to see some support before factoring him in.
runners.comment: Shown very little at big prices in three runs over shorter; would want to see some support

runners.name: Highland Harvey
runners.spotlight: Regressive, ex-Irish maiden who ran well a couple of times for Denis Quinn on Tapeta early in the year; not a bad debut for his third yard, back on turf after four months off, nine days ago and is capable at this level.
runners.comment: Not a bad debut for his third yard, back on turf, nine days ago; capable at this level

runners.name: Dupont Circle
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices and well beaten in three runs to date (7f-1m); this more realistic and much will hinge on the new trip (stamina far from assured on pedigree).
runners.comment: This is more realistic; much will hinge on new trip (stamina far from assured on pedigree)
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Sweetstevie
runners.spotlight: Much better on his turf debut (soft), upped to 1m2f in first-time headgear, when second to a better-than-class winner in a Yarmouth 0-50 11 days ago; different trip, ground and course tonight but that gave something to build on.
runners.comment: Much better on his turf debut (soft), upped to 1m2f in first-time headgear; respected

races.race_name: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap

runners.name: Joycean Way
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who hasn't looked to see it out in a few previous tries at this trip, including over C&D this time last year; back in form here last time and this looks weaker again, given a second try in cheekpieces (in place of blinkers); stamina the question.
runners.comment: Back in form here last time and this looks weaker again; stamina the question
runners.owner: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

runners.name: Dash Of Class
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite on firm ground at Bath in April but has otherwise shown enough in the hood to suggest this mark is within range; high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Has shown enough in the hood to suggest this mark is within range; high on the shortlist

runners.name: Beryl's Girl
runners.spotlight: Gone the wrong way in low-grade 1m handicaps this season and hasn't looked to be crying out for a stiffer test (dam did her winning over 1m and has produced two winners over shorter); now tried in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Gone the wrong way in 1m handicaps this season; stamina not assured in first-time headgear

runners.name: Diamond Aura
runners.spotlight: Longer trip should be within range on pedigree but she pulled hard on her first crack at 1m last time (turf debut) and is still to get involved in a finish after five starts; not easy to find any positives.
runners.comment: Still to get involved in a finish after five starts; not easy to find any positives

runners.name: Dora Vita
runners.spotlight: Exposed, regressive maiden who finished well beaten on her turf debut four weeks ago; bred to be a sprinter and stamina is a concern on this first run at beyond 1m.
runners.comment: Exposed, regressive maiden who finished well beaten on her turf debut; stamina a concern

runners.name: D Day Major Winter
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent maiden who's been off for nine months since running well below market expectations a couple of times; new, jumps-oriented trainer still to get on the board with Flat runners; interesting to see how he goes in the betting.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent maiden who's been off for nine months; watch the market

races.race_name: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts "Confined" Handicap (For horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)

runners.name: Tea Sea
runners.spotlight: Win (off 2lb lower last August) and two seconds to his name from four runs over C&D; pulled hard and didn't see it out over 1m here last time, and this hold-up performer should fare better as long as it doesn't become tactical.
runners.comment: Win (off 2lb lower) and two seconds to his name from four runs over C&D; shortlisted

runners.name: The Bitter Moose
runners.spotlight: Small-field 6f affair not ideal last time and this is a bit easier back in the visor for the first time this season; last summer's easy Epsom win off 4lb lower (made all) showed he's at home round switchback tracks and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: This is a bit easier back in the visor; at home round switchback tracks; one to consider

runners.name: King Of War
runners.spotlight: Three wins, three seconds and only twice out of the first four in 14 runs over C&D; a solid comeback from the front over 1m here 13 days ago (Tea Sea behind), back from seven months off, should have set him up nicely; decent claims.
runners.comment: Solid comeback here 13 days ago, back from seven months off, should have set him up nicely

runners.name: Middleton View
runners.spotlight: Handicapper quick to ease off and he offered more back on turf last time, on just his second run at beyond sprint trips; lacked consistency to date but that gave something to build on.
runners.comment: More back on turf latest; lacked consistency to date but that gave something to build on

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Not had a lot of luck with one thing and another lately and won't mind going back up to an easy 7f as he bids to take his course record to 3-3; higher in the weights than when twice winning easily here for Tom Clover last backend but he's respected.
runners.comment: Won't mind going back up to an easy 7f as he bids to take his course record to 3-3; player
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Lunario
runners.spotlight: Dual winner from the front last summer, latterly off 9lb higher; completely lost the plot this season so, while this is easier dropped another 4lb, he does have it to prove, especially with an easy 7f a bare minimum.
runners.comment: Lost the plot this season; has it to prove, especially with an easy 7f a bare minimum

races.race_name: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Havana Mojito
runners.spotlight: Straightforward three-time course winner who looked like making it four (first-time blinkers, switched from cheekpieces) until nabbed late by Aim For The Bull late last month; in off the same mark and his claims are obvious back down in trip.
runners.comment: Straightforward three-time course winner; claims are obvious back down in trip

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Fine on all surfaces and has enjoyed a consistent spell since the cheekpieces returned, culminating in a 1m win here (beat Havana Mojito narrowly; 2lb worse off) a few weeks ago; an easy 7f is a bare minimum but he enters calculations.
runners.comment: Consistent since cheekpieces returned; easy 7f a bare minimum but he enters calculations

runners.name: Maury
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent, hard-pulling front-runner who's been beaten a long way in five runs on turf, latterly when tailed off at Lingfield last month; other pace in here and he's some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Inconsistent, hard-pulling front-runner who's been beaten a long way in five runs on turf

runners.name: Oh So Audacious
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner here for Harry Eustace, latterly off this mark over C&D last August; entitled to have come on for her recent comeback from seven months off for her new yard; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Three-time winner here for Harry Eustace, latterly off this mark; each-way shout

runners.name: Bated Breeze
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden (5f-7f) who's been tried in two forms of headgear; pulled hard and hit traffic on his 6f comeback here but he usually over-races and can miss the kick, so will never come without risk.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden; usually over-races and can miss the kick; will never come without risk

runners.name: Henry Tudor
runners.spotlight: Popped up in a Polytrack 0-50 classified event in the winter but well held twice since and hasn't achieved a lot in three runs at beyond sprint distances; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Not achieved a lot in three runs at beyond sprint distances; hard to recommend

runners.name: Daley T
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's run exclusively in sprints to date; first-time cheekpieces brought about an improved showing in a 0-50 classified event here last time but he's no certainty to repeat the effort, especially jumping up from 5f.
runners.comment: Better in 0-50 classified event here latest; no certainty to repeat the effort up from 5f

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Two wins, two near-misses and two fourths to his name from six runs over C&D and he's enjoyed a revival of late; more on his plate off his revised mark but he hasn't been forced up in class and has every chance of the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Revival of late; more on his plate off his revised mark but every chance of the hat-trick

runners.name: Prefer The Sister
runners.spotlight: Regressed for Matt Crawley before sold for just 2,200gns in April; started favourite for her new yard (6f, soft) 11 days ago, when second despite fluffing the start, and has claims off the same mark back up in trip (stays 1m).
runners.comment: Favourite for new yard (6f, soft) 11 days ago; second despite fluffing the start; claims

runners.name: Bear To Dream
runners.spotlight: Not as consistent in recent times but rarely fails to give her running round here; little room for manoeuvre off this mark but a good test back down in trip will be fine and she holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Likes it here; good test back down in trip will be fine and she holds each-way claims

runners.name: Amathus
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who traditionally peaks in the summer; showed enough in three runs over 1m round Bath in the spring to suggest he's still up to winning at this level if he gets one of his better starts and the race pans out his way.
runners.comment: Still up to winning at this level if he gets away on terms and the race pans out his way

runners.name: Zu Run
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 11lb higher last spring whose mark tumbled on the AW over the winter; back in form when second to Dion Baker here last time, in headgear (retained) for the first time in two years, and a 7lb swing entitles him to at least close the gap.
runners.comment: Back in form when second to Dion Baker; 7lb swing entitles him to at least close the gap
